title: The Impossible Target
description: 🚫 The magazine doesn't have the required letter at all.

title: The Shortage
description: 📉 The magazine has the right letter, but not enough copies of it.

title: The Abundant Supply
description: 📦 The magazine has more than enough letters to cover the required duplicates.

title: The Exact Anagram
description: 🧩 The magazine is an exact scramble of the ransom note with no leftover letters.

title: The Length Mismatch
description: 📏 The ransom note is longer than the magazine, making it instantly impossible.

title: The Substring Extraction
description: ✂️ The ransom note letters are scattered seamlessly throughout a larger word.

title: The Alphabet Soup
description: 🥣 The magazine contains the entire alphabet, easily fulfilling a small, unique note.

title: The 'Off-By-One' Illusion
description: 👀 The magazine looks like it has everything, but it is sneakily missing exactly one duplicate character.

title: The Needle in the Haystack
description: 🌾 The magazine is enormous, but it is missing the one extremely rare character needed for the note.

title: The Decoy Overload
description: 🗑️ The magazine has thousands of useless decoy characters, forcing the algorithm to sift through massive noise to find the exact matches.

title: High Frequency Duplicates (Success)
description: 👯‍♂️ The note requires a massive cluster of just a few characters. The magazine has them perfectly scrambled.

title: High Frequency Duplicates (Failure)
description: 📉 The magazine is packed with characters, but is short by exactly one 'a' in a massive cluster.

title: The Disjoint Sets
description: 🧱 The strings are exactly the same length, but share absolutely zero characters. (Tests if the algorithm fails fast).

title: The Reverse Alphabetical Trap
description: 🔤 The magazine is sorted backwards, while the note is sorted forwards, ensuring any early-exit or sorting assumptions are thoroughly tested.
